target.name: Verkor

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: KoBold Metals
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $2.96B

private.name: Electric Hydrogen
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

private.name: Amogy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $700M

data.narrative:
Verkor is a Series C climate and energy company that has raised €3B+ to date. The valuation peer set comprises 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (LNZA), with primary focus on three same-stage, same-sector Series C companies: KoBold Metals ($3.0B), Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B), and Amogy ($700M).

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach, given sufficient Series C comparables in the Climate & Energy sector. The median valuation across the three peers is $1.0B, with observed range spanning $700M to $3.0B. This $1.0B median reflects the central tendency of contemporaneous fundraising activity among direct-stage competitors and serves as the primary quantitative anchor.

The triangulated valuation range is $850M–$1.1B (median $1.0B). This relatively narrow band reflects reliance on a single method and a limited peer set of three companies. Key uncertainties center on company-specific operational metrics not present in this dataset—unit economics, technology differentiation, deployment timeline, and capital intensity relative to peers. The $2.3B spread observed in the broader peer universe ($700M to $3.0B) underscores material variance in how market participants value frontier energy companies at this stage. Additional valuation methods (DCF, revenue multiples, or comparable M&A transactions) would strengthen conviction in this range.
